title: Pixel-level editing in screenshots
description: Screenshots that have been edited using image manipulation tools like Photoshop or GIMP to alter text, numbers, or UI elements. Common in fabricating chat messages, financial records, or system outputs.
indicators: Inconsistent font rendering, color mismatches near edited areas, sharp edges around pasted elements, misaligned UI components, irregular pixel patterns at edit boundaries

title: Browser DevTools content modification
description: Using browser developer tools to modify displayed content before taking a screenshot. Changes are made to the DOM without altering the actual data, producing screenshots that show false information.
indicators: Perfect font consistency (since fonts are real), no pixel artifacts, but content may be inconsistent with expected system behavior or formatting patterns

title: Screenshot timestamp manipulation
description: Altering system clock before taking a screenshot to fake the timestamp, or editing the timestamp area of a screenshot to show a different time.
indicators: Timezone inconsistencies, timestamp format mismatches with the alleged OS or browser, daylight saving time errors, metadata timestamp vs. displayed timestamp mismatch

title: Composite screenshot creation
description: Combining multiple screenshots or adding elements from different sources to create a single fabricated image that never existed as a genuine screen capture.
indicators: Resolution differences between regions, DPI inconsistencies, different anti-aliasing patterns, lighting/brightness variations across the image, inconsistent window chrome elements

title: EXIF metadata stripping
description: Removing EXIF metadata from image files to hide information about the original capture device, software used, or timestamps. Common when trying to disguise the origin of an image.
indicators: Complete absence of EXIF data in formats that typically contain it (JPEG, TIFF), file size inconsistent with expected metadata overhead, no camera/device information

title: Log entry deletion
description: Selectively removing log entries to hide specific events or activities. The remaining log shows gaps in sequence numbers, timestamps, or connection patterns.
indicators: Gaps in sequential line numbers, timestamp jumps, missing entries in otherwise continuous sequences, file size reduction, irregular line spacing patterns

title: Log entry modification
description: Altering existing log entries to change usernames, IP addresses, timestamps, or status codes. The modified entries may have subtly different formatting from authentic entries.
indicators: Inconsistent field formatting, unusual character encoding, modified timestamp patterns, field length anomalies, whitespace irregularities

title: Synthetic log generation
description: Creating entirely fake log files using scripts or templates. These logs may appear too perfect, lacking the natural variation seen in genuine system output.
indicators: Too-regular timestamp intervals, repetitive patterns, missing natural jitter in response times, perfectly sequential request IDs, identical error message strings

title: Log file backdating
description: Modifying file system timestamps to make log files appear older or newer than they actually are. Often done by changing system clock before file creation or using file attribute modification tools.
indicators: File creation timestamp after modification timestamp, metadata timestamps inconsistent with file content timestamps, filesystem journal inconsistencies

title: Log injection attacks
description: Injecting false log entries into a legitimate log file to fabricate evidence of events that never occurred. The injected content may have slightly different formatting.
indicators: Encoding differences between injected and original entries, irregular log level patterns, entries that break established logging format conventions, unusual source identifiers

title: EXIF data fabrication
description: Creating fake EXIF metadata for images, including false GPS coordinates, camera model information, or timestamps. Tools like ExifTool can be used to add or modify any EXIF field.
indicators: Unrealistic GPS coordinates for claimed location, camera model inconsistent with image quality, EXIF version inconsistencies, impossible lens/sensor combinations

title: Document property manipulation
description: Changing document properties like author, creation date, modification date, or revision history in Office documents or PDFs to misrepresent document origin or timeline.
indicators: Author field inconsistent with document style, revision number too low for document complexity, timezone mismatches in embedded timestamps, font availability date vs. creation date

title: File hash collision attempt
description: Attempting to create a modified file that produces the same hash as the original, making the tampered version appear authentic in hash-based verification systems.
indicators: File content inconsistencies despite matching hash, unusual file structure or padding, appended data blocks, abnormal file size for content type

title: Multiple re-saves and compression artifacts
description: Images that have been saved multiple times, especially in lossy formats like JPEG, accumulate compression artifacts. Excessive artifacts in specific regions may indicate selective editing.
indicators: High Error Level Analysis (ELA) differences between regions, visible JPEG blocking artifacts at different levels, ghost images from previous edits, quality inconsistencies

title: Screenshot from virtual machine or emulator
description: Screenshots taken from virtual machines or device emulators that simulate real environments. These can be used to fabricate evidence of system states that never existed on real hardware.
indicators: VM-specific UI elements (menu bar items, taskbar indicators), virtualization driver names in system info, unusual screen resolutions, MAC address patterns typical of VMs

title: AI-generated content insertion
description: Using AI tools (like ChatGPT, Midjourney) to generate realistic-looking text, images, or data that is then inserted into evidence. The AI-generated content may have subtle artifacts.
indicators: Unnaturally perfect grammar in supposedly user-generated content, AI watermarks, statistical anomalies in text patterns, too-smooth gradients in generated images

title: Selective cropping to hide context
description: Cropping screenshots or images to remove surrounding context that would reveal the evidence is misleading or shows only part of the full picture.
indicators: Awkward crop boundaries, missing standard UI elements (status bar, scrollbar), truncated text, URL bar absent from browser screenshots, uneven margins

title: File format conversion to remove evidence
description: Converting files between formats (e.g., TIFF to JPEG, PNG to WebP) to strip metadata, remove editing history, or eliminate format-specific forensic markers.
indicators: Format-specific metadata missing that should be present, unusual format choice for the type of content, loss of expected color depth, compression artifacts inconsistent with claimed source

title: Chain of custody verification
description: Proper digital evidence handling requires maintaining a documented chain of custody showing who handled the evidence, when, and what actions were performed.
indicators: Verified chain of custody documentation, consistent hash values across custody transfers, write-protected storage media, timestamped access logs

title: Hash-based integrity verification
description: Computing and storing cryptographic hashes (SHA-256, SHA-512) at the time of evidence collection to later verify that the evidence has not been modified.
indicators: Matching SHA-256 hashes between collection and analysis, documented hash values in evidence reports, multiple hash algorithm verification

title: Original file preservation
description: Best practice requires preserving the original evidence file in its unmodified state, performing all analysis on forensic copies rather than the original.
indicators: Bit-for-bit forensic copies used for analysis, read-only access to originals, documented copy procedures, verified copy integrity via hashing

title: Mobile screenshot fabrication
description: Creating fake mobile screenshots using mock-up tools or editing apps. These often involve fabricating chat conversations, notification screens, or app interfaces.
indicators: Wrong status bar format for claimed OS version, incorrect carrier/WiFi icon rendering, font inconsistencies with actual mobile OS, notification badge count anomalies

title: Selective log export manipulation
description: Exporting logs from a system but selectively filtering or modifying entries during the export process. The exported log appears genuine but is incomplete.
indicators: Missing common log entries (startup, shutdown), gaps in otherwise continuous monitoring, missing correlated entries (request without response), unusual export format

title: GPS coordinate spoofing in images
description: Adding false GPS coordinates to image metadata to claim the photo was taken at a location where the photographer was never present.
indicators: GPS precision too high or consistent (exact coordinates), inconsistent altitude data, timezone metadata conflicts with GPS location, lighting/shadows inconsistent with claimed location

title: Inconsistent rendering engine artifacts
description: Screenshots showing content rendered by a different browser or application than claimed. Each rendering engine leaves distinct patterns in font rendering, spacing, and element display.
indicators: Font smoothing inconsistent with claimed browser, subpixel rendering differences, CSS rendering anomalies, scrollbar style mismatches with OS
